note: Profiles generated fresh from lib/personas/roster.json for cron-explainer. Each persona keeps its canonical role/tech/stack/temperament; 'motivation' is why THIS person lands on a cron explainer in their real workflow.

personas.name: Priya
personas.stack: terminal + neovim, GitHub, Kubernetes dashboards, Datadog, Linear, Slack
personas.temperament: skeptical of new tools; hates signups; will inspect network tab if suspicious; abandons anything slower than a CLI
personas.motivation: Reviewing a teammate's PR that adds a CronJob to a K8s manifest with `*/15 9-17 * * 1-5`; wants to confirm at a glance it really means business-hours-weekdays before approving, and to sanity-check the next run times in her own timezone.

personas.name: Marcus
personas.stack: VS Code, GitHub, Vercel, Figma dev mode, Storybook, Discord/Slack
personas.temperament: enthusiastic early adopter; shares tools in team Slack when impressed; notices janky CSS instantly
personas.motivation: Setting up a GitHub Actions scheduled workflow and a Vercel cron; never remembers cron syntax, usually googles crontab.guru — wants to type 'every weekday at 9am' and get the expression instead of hand-building it.

personas.name: Wen
personas.stack: BigQuery/SQL, Google Sheets, Looker Studio, dbt, GA4, Slack
personas.temperament: lives in data hygiene; distrusts tools that transform data invisibly; wants CSV in/out everywhere
personas.motivation: Schedules dbt Cloud jobs and Airflow DAGs with cron; wants to verify a `0 6 * * *` job actually fires at 6am her timezone (not UTC) before a stakeholder asks why the dashboard was stale.

personas.name: Tomás
personas.role: Operations analyst at a mid-size company
personas.stack: Excel (power user), Tableau, Jira, Teams, SharePoint
personas.temperament: pragmatic; IT blocks installs so browser tools appeal; wary of pasting company data into random sites
personas.motivation: Inherited a server with crontab entries he didn't write; needs to read what `30 2 1 * *` does without learning cron, and a browser tool he can't install-block is exactly what he can use at work.

personas.name: Dana
personas.device: MacBook in cafes; phone between meetings
personas.stack: HubSpot, GA4, Notion, Canva, LinkedIn Campaign Manager, Google Ads
personas.temperament: ruthless about time; if value isn't obvious in one scroll she's gone; screenshots tools she likes for the team channel
personas.motivation: Her marketing-automation platform asks for a 'cron schedule' for a recurring report export; she has no idea what to type and just wants to describe 'every Monday at 8am' and copy the answer.

personas.name: Jules
personas.stack: Notion, Buffer, Mailchimp, Figma (consuming), X/LinkedIn/Mastodon, Discord
personas.temperament: posts constantly, juggles platform quirks daily; allergic to anything requiring a login for a small job
personas.motivation: Configuring a self-hosted scheduler (Uptime Kuma / a webhook bot) that wants cron; lands here to translate 'every 6 hours on weekends' without making an account.

personas.name: Aisha
personas.stack: Figma, FigJam, Notion, Loom, Maze, Slack
personas.temperament: judges craft hard — spacing, copy tone, empty states; a clumsy UI loses her regardless of utility; advocates loudly for tools that feel considered
personas.motivation: A developer handed her a cron string in a spec for a scheduled design-system sync; she wants to understand what it means in plain English and judges whether this little tool is crafted enough to trust and recommend.

personas.name: Rob
personas.stack: Figma, Illustrator, Photoshop, Google Drive, client Slacks/emails
personas.temperament: does repetitive asset-prep grunt work weekly; price-sensitive; compares everything to 'I could do this in Photoshop in 4 minutes'
personas.motivation: Set up a cron-based backup script for his client deliverables once and forgot the syntax; comes back occasionally to decode `0 */4 * * *` and confirm when the next backup runs.

personas.name: Elena
personas.device: laptop + phone, half the day in meetings
personas.stack: Google Calendar (wall-to-wall), Slack, Linear, Google Docs, Zoom, dashboards she skims
personas.temperament: 30-second patience budget; uses tools between meetings on her phone; anything requiring setup is dead to her; recommends tools that save her reports time
personas.motivation: In an incident review someone blames a misconfigured cron; she wants to paste the expression on her phone and instantly see whether it ran when they claimed, without pinging an engineer.

personas.name: Sam
personas.stack: Notion, Sheets, Slack, Zoom, Asana, Amplitude (skims), endless browser tabs
personas.temperament: synthesizes and shares constantly — exports, links, screenshots; values anything that makes him look organized; won't debug anything
personas.motivation: Writing a ticket that specifies a recurring job; wants to paste a cron expression into the ticket AND include a plain-English gloss + a shareable link so engineers and stakeholders both understand the schedule.
